source_paper_title: Many-Server Asymptotics for Join-the-Shortest-Queue in the Super-Halfin-Whitt Scaling Window
context:
Section 2, Remark 2.9 (page 11 in the provided parsed text; PDF page 10/73–11/73 boundary)

It will be an interesting future direction to identify the precise scaling of the system load where this transition of behavior occurs.
title: Identify the critical load-scaling threshold where JSQ matches the centralized \(M/M/N\) steady-state limit
latex:
Consider, for each integer \(N\ge 1\), a system of \(N\) identical single-server queues with unit-mean exponential service times and a single dispatcher. Jobs arrive according to a Poisson process of rate \(\lambda(N)\) and are routed according to the Join-the-Shortest-Queue (JSQ) policy (ties broken arbitrarily). Let \(S^{(N)}(\infty)\) denote the total number of jobs in steady state (i.e., under the stationary distribution of the Markov process of queue occupancies), and let \(I^{(N)}(\infty)\) denote the number of idle servers in steady state.

Assume a heavy-traffic sequence with per-server load
\[
\lambda_N := \frac{\lambda(N)}{N} = 1 - \frac{\beta}{N^{\alpha}},\qquad \beta>0,\ \alpha>0,
\]
so that \(\lambda(N)=N-\beta N^{1-\alpha}\). Define the centered-and-scaled steady-state total-job count
\[
X^{(N)}_{\alpha} := \frac{S^{(N)}(\infty)-N}{N^{\alpha}}.
\]
Also consider the corresponding centralized \(M/M/N\) queue with the same arrival rate \(\lambda(N)\) and service rate \(1\) at each server, and let \(\widetilde S^{(N)}(\infty)\) denote its steady-state total number of jobs; define
\[
\widetilde X^{(N)}_{\alpha} := \frac{\widetilde S^{(N)}(\infty)-N}{N^{\alpha}}.
\]
It is known that in certain regimes (e.g., sufficiently large \(\alpha\)) the JSQ and centralized systems have the same limiting distribution after appropriate scaling, while in other regimes they do not.

Problem: Determine the sharp threshold \(\alpha_c\in(0,\infty)\) (and, if needed, the correct refined scaling when \(\alpha=\alpha_c\)) such that:
- if \(\alpha>\alpha_c\), then \(X^{(N)}_{\alpha}\) and \(\widetilde X^{(N)}_{\alpha}\) converge in distribution to the same non-degenerate limit as \(N\to\infty\);
- if \(\alpha<\alpha_c\), then the weak limits of \(X^{(N)}_{\alpha}\) and \(\widetilde X^{(N)}_{\alpha}\) are different (or one of them fails to have the same scaling limit);
and characterize the limiting law(s) in the threshold case \(\alpha=\alpha_c\).
